title: European defence supplier dependency intelligence platform
reason: EDIS pushes industrial readiness, but the supply base is fragmented. A structured dependency graph for suppliers, bottlenecks, capabilities and national/EU fit can serve primes, investors and public agencies.

title: EDF / EDIP proposal and consortium workspace for SMEs
reason: EDF/EUDIS/EDIP opportunities are hard for SMEs to navigate. A workspace that matches capabilities, partners, eligibility, calls and documentation can lower barriers into defence innovation.

title: Counter-UAS command and evidence console for critical infrastructure
reason: Drone threats are rising across military and civilian critical sites. A software layer for detection evidence, incident workflow, integration and compliance can avoid building weapons while serving real demand.

title: Secure combat-cloud connector layer for European defence systems
reason: Airbus and European air-combat concepts point toward multi-domain combat cloud and interoperable C2. The gap is not only platforms; it is secure connectors, schemas, adapters and audit layers.

title: Defence export-control and classified-compliance OS
reason: European defence/dual-use companies face export controls, classified handling, cybersecurity and procurement eligibility. A software OS for compliance evidence and workflows is accessible and B2B-heavy.

title: Ammunition and energetics supply-chain observability
reason: Ammunition/energetics is a red bottleneck. A non-weapon software layer for capacity, supplier risk, lead times, materials and production readiness can support primes and ministries.

title: Fleet readiness and predictive maintenance platform for defence assets
reason: European forces need higher readiness from existing assets. Maintenance data, spares visibility, lifecycle support and predictive alerts are software-accessible compared with building platforms.

title: Space ISR tasking and fusion layer for maritime/security users
reason: Europe has space ISR actors and secure connectivity programmes. A layer for tasking, fusing and delivering maritime/geospatial intelligence can serve public security and defence-adjacent users.

title: Synthetic training and mission rehearsal toolkit for European SMEs
reason: EDF highlights simulation, multi-domain operations and critical technologies. SMEs/primes need reusable simulation/training components without rebuilding bespoke environments every time.

title: Defence procurement radar for founders and investors
reason: Defence opportunities are scattered across EU, national, NATO and prime channels. A radar that tracks calls, tenders, capability gaps, supplier needs and fit scores can help founders position intelligently.

title: Dual-use startup security-clearance and procurement readiness pathway
reason: Promising dual-use startups hit friction around security, procurement, documentation, pilots and trust. A guided readiness pathway can prepare them for primes and public buyers.

title: European interoperability test harness for defence software
reason: Fragmentation and interoperability are central defence pain points. A test harness that validates schemas, APIs, data exchange, audit logs and security controls could serve C2, ISR and combat-cloud teams.
